text.title: Quality Attribute Scenario Structure
text.summary: 6-part scenario anatomy with worked examples for performance, availability, and security.

rule.statement: Every quality attribute scenario must have exactly 6 parts: Source, Stimulus, Environment, Artifact, Response, Response Measure. A scenario missing any part is invalid.
rule.rationale: Without all 6 parts, the scenario is ambiguous: "must be fast" cannot be tested or verified. A complete scenario generates a specific fitness function (load test, chaos drill, security scan).

rule.statement: The Response Measure must be quantitative with a unit: "p99 latency < 500ms", "0 errors during 10-minute spike", "scale to 2x instances within 60 seconds". Qualitative measures ("acceptable", "good") are rejected.
rule.rationale: Qualitative measures cannot be automated into CI gates. Without a number, the scenario cannot fail a test and therefore cannot drive architectural decisions.

rule.statement: Every (H,H) scenario (High importance, High difficulty) must have an executable fitness function wired into CI/CD before the scenario is declared complete.
rule.rationale: A scenario without a test is decoration. The fitness function is the mechanism that prevents architectural regression as the system evolves.

list.item: Source: Who or what generates the stimulus (user, external system, attacker, time-based trigger)
Stimulus: The event or condition that occurs (request, failure, attack, data volume)
Environment: Context under which it occurs (normal operation, peak load, degraded mode, under attack)
Artifact: Which part of the system is affected (API gateway, checkout service, database, entire platform)
Response: How the system should behave in reaction (auto-scale, queue requests, return cached data, reject unauthorized)
Response Measure: Quantifiable criterion for success (p99 < 500ms, 99.99% uptime, 0 unauthorized data reads)

example.note: Performance scenario: Source=Marketing campaign, Stimulus=10x traffic spike (1M concurrent users), Environment=Production peak hours, Artifact=API gateway + checkout service, Response=Auto-scale + queue excess requests, Measure=p99 latency < 500ms, 0% errors, scale within 60s

Availability scenario: Source=AWS AZ failure, Stimulus=AZ us-east-1a goes down, Environment=Production normal operation, Artifact=Application tier, Response=Traffic shifts to remaining AZs automatically, Measure=Recovery within 30s, < 0.01% requests dropped

Security scenario: Source=External attacker, Stimulus=Credential stuffing attack (10K login attempts/minute), Environment=Production, Artifact=Auth service, Response=Rate limiting + account lockout + alert, Measure=100% of brute-force sessions blocked, alert within 60s

text.reference: CI script that rejects incomplete or qualitative scenarios
